title: 7Li+d -> 2alpha+n reaction Experimental results at Ed=1.0 MeV

x4freetext: 7Li+d -> 2alpha+n reaction
Experimental results at Ed=1.0 MeV

x4codes.hlp: Universita degli Studi di Messina, Messina, Italy
INFN Laboratori Nazionali del Sud, Catania, Italy
Universita degli Studi di Catania + INFN Catania, Italy
Centro Sicil.di Fisica Nucl.CSFNSM, Catania, Italy

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Nuovo Cimento A, Vol.22, p.313 (1974)

x4codes.hlp: Universita degli Studi di Catania + INFN Catania, Italy

x4freetext: 2.7 MV
Two movable surface barrier detectors supplied
by ORTEC. One of the detectors was fixed at an
angle of 88.4 degr., while the second one was
allowed to rotate between 30 and  110 degrees.
One of the detectors remained in a fixed position and
acted also as a beam monitor.
A natural Li target 0.5 mg/cm2 thick, evaporated on a
polyvinilformal backing of 0.1 um was used.
Systematic error

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: No information about source of uncertainty
Reaction records were changed in subentries
003,004,005. In sub3 FLAG was deleted and negative
values of polar angle are given for the azimuth angle
of 180 degrees.
Error designations in sub 001-005 were
changed.
SD: Small corrections in all Subents.

x4headers.headerHelp: Energy of incident projectile, laboratory system
Total systematic uncertainty

x4freetext: Internal energy spectra is given. Relative motion of
alpha-n rest system is considered (not lab. system nor
projectile-target centre-of-mass system).
ANG1 is the angle of detection for the "first" alpha
particle, ANG2 is the polar angle of the "second"
emitted alpha particle in the system where the
alpha-neutron complex is at rest.
is the internal energy of the alpha-n system

Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: Relative motion of alpha-n rest system is considered
(not lab. system nor projectile-target centre-of-mass
system).
ANG1 is an angle of alpha particle detection.
ANG2 is a relative angle that can be interpreted as
the angle of the alpha-particle in the centre of mass
of the alpha-n system when the motion of this latter is
described in the system of the centre of mass of the
three particles.
is the internal energy of the alpha-n system
